speaker: BEDFORD
text: ’Fore God, his Grace is bold to trust these traitors.

speaker: EXETER
text: They shall be apprehended by and by.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: How smooth and even they do bear themselves,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: As if allegiance in their bosoms sat

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Crownèd with faith and constant loyalty.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: The King hath note of all that they intend,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: By interception which they dream not of.

speaker: EXETER
text: Nay, but the man that was his bedfellow,

speaker: EXETER
text: Whom he hath dulled and cloyed with gracious favors—

speaker: EXETER
text: That he should, for a foreign purse, so sell

speaker: EXETER
text: His sovereign’s life to death and treachery!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now sits the wind fair, and we will aboard.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My Lord of Cambridge, and my kind Lord of Masham,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And you, my gentle knight, give me your thoughts.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Think you not that the powers we bear with us

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will cut their passage through the force of France,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Doing the execution and the act

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For which we have in head assembled them?

speaker: SCROOP
text: No doubt, my liege, if each man do his best.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I doubt not that, since we are well persuaded

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We carry not a heart with us from hence

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That grows not in a fair consent with ours,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor leave not one behind that doth not wish

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Success and conquest to attend on us.

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Never was monarch better feared and loved

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Than is your Majesty. There’s not, I think, a subject

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: That sits in heart-grief and uneasiness

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Under the sweet shade of your government.

speaker: GREY
text: True. Those that were your father’s enemies

speaker: GREY
text: Have steeped their galls in honey, and do serve you

speaker: GREY
text: With hearts create of duty and of zeal.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We therefore have great cause of thankfulness,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And shall forget the office of our hand

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sooner than quittance of desert and merit

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: According to the weight and worthiness.

speaker: SCROOP
text: So service shall with steelèd sinews toil,

speaker: SCROOP
text: And labor shall refresh itself with hope

speaker: SCROOP
text: To do your Grace incessant services.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We judge no less.—Uncle of Exeter,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Enlarge the man committed yesterday

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That railed against our person. We consider

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It was excess of wine that set him on,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And on his more advice we pardon him.

speaker: SCROOP
text: That’s mercy, but too much security.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Let him be punished, sovereign, lest example

speaker: SCROOP
text: Breed, by his sufferance, more of such a kind.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, let us yet be merciful.

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: So may your Highness, and yet punish too.

speaker: GREY
text: Sir, you show great mercy if you give him life

speaker: GREY
text: After the taste of much correction.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Alas, your too much love and care of me

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Are heavy orisons ’gainst this poor wretch.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If little faults proceeding on distemper

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall not be winked at, how shall we stretch our eye

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When capital crimes, chewed, swallowed, and digested,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Appear before us? We’ll yet enlarge that man,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Though Cambridge, Scroop, and Grey, in their dear care

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And tender preservation of our person,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Would have him punished. And now to our French causes.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Who are the late commissioners?

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: I one, my lord.

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Your Highness bade me ask for it today.

speaker: SCROOP
text: So did you me, my liege.

speaker: GREY
text: And I, my royal sovereign.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then Richard, Earl of Cambridge, there is yours—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: There yours, Lord Scroop of Masham.—And, sir knight,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Grey of Northumberland, this same is yours.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Read them, and know I know your worthiness.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My Lord of Westmoreland and uncle Exeter,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We will aboard tonight.—Why how now, gentlemen?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What see you in those papers, that you lose

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So much complexion?—Look you, how they change.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Their cheeks are paper.—Why, what read you there

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That have so cowarded and chased your blood

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Out of appearance?

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: I do confess my fault,

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: And do submit me to your Highness’ mercy.

speaker:
GREY
              /
              SCROOP
text: To which we all appeal.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The mercy that was quick in us but late

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By your own counsel is suppressed and killed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You must not dare, for shame, to talk of mercy,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For your own reasons turn into your bosoms

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As dogs upon their masters, worrying you.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: See you, my princes and my noble peers,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: These English monsters. My Lord of Cambridge here,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You know how apt our love was to accord

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To furnish him with all appurtenants

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Belonging to his honor, and this man

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hath, for a few light crowns, lightly conspired

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And sworn unto the practices of France

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To kill us here in Hampton; to the which

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This knight, no less for bounty bound to us

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than Cambridge is, hath likewise sworn.—But O,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What shall I say to thee, Lord Scroop, thou cruel,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ingrateful, savage, and inhuman creature?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou that didst bear the key of all my counsels,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That knew’st the very bottom of my soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That almost mightst have coined me into gold,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wouldst thou have practiced on me for thy use—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: May it be possible that foreign hire

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Could out of thee extract one spark of evil

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That might annoy my finger? ’Tis so strange

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That, though the truth of it stands off as gross

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As black and white, my eye will scarcely see it.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Treason and murder ever kept together,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As two yoke-devils sworn to either’s purpose,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Working so grossly in a natural cause

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That admiration did not whoop at them.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But thou, ’gainst all proportion, didst bring in

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wonder to wait on treason and on murder,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And whatsoever cunning fiend it was

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That wrought upon thee so preposterously

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hath got the voice in hell for excellence.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: All other devils that suggest by treasons

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do botch and bungle up damnation

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With patches, colors, and with forms being fetched

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From glist’ring semblances of piety;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But he that tempered thee bade thee stand up,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Gave thee no instance why thou shouldst do treason,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Unless to dub thee with the name of traitor.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If that same demon that hath gulled thee thus

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Should with his lion gait walk the whole world,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He might return to vasty Tartar back

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And tell the legions “I can never win

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A soul so easy as that Englishman’s.”

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, how hast thou with jealousy infected

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The sweetness of affiance! Show men dutiful?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, so didst thou. Seem they grave and learnèd?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, so didst thou. Come they of noble family?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, so didst thou. Seem they religious?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, so didst thou. Or are they spare in diet,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Free from gross passion or of mirth or anger,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Constant in spirit, not swerving with the blood,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Garnished and decked in modest complement,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not working with the eye without the ear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And but in purgèd judgment trusting neither?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such and so finely bolted didst thou seem.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thus thy fall hath left a kind of blot

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To mark the full-fraught man and best endued

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With some suspicion. I will weep for thee,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For this revolt of thine methinks is like

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Another fall of man.—Their faults are open.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Arrest them to the answer of the law,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And God acquit them of their practices.

speaker: EXETER
text: I arrest thee of high treason, by the name of

speaker: EXETER
text: Richard, Earl of Cambridge.—

speaker: EXETER
text: I arrest thee of high treason, by the name of

speaker: EXETER
text: Henry, Lord Scroop of Masham.—

speaker: EXETER
text: I arrest thee of high treason, by the name of

speaker: EXETER
text: Thomas Grey, knight, of Northumberland.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Our purposes God justly hath discovered,

speaker: SCROOP
text: And I repent my fault more than my death,

speaker: SCROOP
text: Which I beseech your Highness to forgive,

speaker: SCROOP
text: Although my body pay the price of it.

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: For me, the gold of France did not seduce,

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Although I did admit it as a motive

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: The sooner to effect what I intended;

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: But God be thankèd for prevention,

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Which I in sufferance heartily will rejoice,

speaker: CAMBRIDGE
text: Beseeching God and you to pardon me.

speaker: GREY
text: Never did faithful subject more rejoice

speaker: GREY
text: At the discovery of most dangerous treason

speaker: GREY
text: Than I do at this hour joy o’er myself,

speaker: GREY
text: Prevented from a damnèd enterprise.

speaker: GREY
text: My fault, but not my body, pardon, sovereign.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: God quit you in His mercy. Hear your sentence:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You have conspired against our royal person,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Joined with an enemy proclaimed, and from his coffers

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Received the golden earnest of our death,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wherein you would have sold your king to slaughter,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His princes and his peers to servitude,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His subjects to oppression and contempt,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And his whole kingdom into desolation.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Touching our person, seek we no revenge,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But we our kingdom’s safety must so tender,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose ruin you have sought, that to her laws

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We do deliver you. Get you therefore hence,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Poor miserable wretches, to your death,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The taste whereof God of His mercy give

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You patience to endure, and true repentance

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of all your dear offenses.—Bear them hence.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now, lords, for France, the enterprise whereof

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall be to you as us, like glorious.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We doubt not of a fair and lucky war,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Since God so graciously hath brought to light

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This dangerous treason lurking in our way

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To hinder our beginnings. We doubt not now

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But every rub is smoothèd on our way.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then forth, dear countrymen. Let us deliver

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Our puissance into the hand of God,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Putting it straight in expedition.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Cheerly to sea. The signs of war advance.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No king of England if not king of France.
